member.name: Kim Johnson

addresses: Liverpool Riverside Office, Kuumba Imani Millenium Centre, 4 Princes Road, Liverpool, L8 1TH

member.synopsis: Kim Johnson is the Labour MP for Liverpool Riverside , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sentencing: Young Offenders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the (1) mean and (2) median tariff length is for people receiving a life sentence aged 16 to 25 at the time of sentencing in each year since 2015 by ethnicity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The following tables show the (1) mean and (2) median tariff lengths for people receiving a life sentence aged 16 to 25 at the time of sentencing, by ethnicity, in each year since 2015.Table 1: Mean tariff at sentence for life sentences by ethnicity and y...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sentencing: Young Offenders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the average sentence length was for those convicted aged 16 to 25 at the time of sentencing for cases included in the CPS Joint Enterprise National Monitoring Scheme 2024-25 by ethnicity and sex.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice publishes data on average sentence length for a variety of offences including murder and attempted murder in England and Wales. These data are available by ethnicity and sex in the Outcomes by Offences data tool, that can be downlo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arms Trade: Military Bases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to help ensure that arms transferred through UK military bases are used in accordance with international law.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK continues to be committed to upholding International Law and will always operate in accordance with its key principals.The use of UK military bases by foreign governments is governed by a range of longstanding agreements. For operational security r...

writtenQuestions.heading: Royal Fleet Auxiliary: Labour Turnover and Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what recent assessment he has made of the progress the Royal Fleet Auxiliary has made in recruiting and retaining civilian seafarer staff since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence keeps the recruitment and retention of Royal Fleet Auxiliary civilian seafarers under close review, including through ongoing workforce analysis and engagement with Maritime Trade Unions. The RFA has faced sustained recruitment and...

writtenQuestions.heading: RFA Fort Victoria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the operational (a) status and (b) future of RFA Fort Victoria.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Royal Fleet Auxiliary (RFA) are an indispensable part of Defence, and I am grateful to all the seafarers in the RFA who support defence outputs. The RFA operates platforms in accordance with the defence priorities. RFA Fort Victoria will be regenerate...

writtenQuestions.heading: RFA Fort Victoria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment has been made of the additional crewing required to bring the RFA Fort Victoria back into operational service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Royal Fleet Auxiliary (RFA) are an indispensable part of Defence, and I am grateful to all the seafarers in the RFA who support defence outputs. The RFA operates platforms in accordance with the defence priorities. RFA Fort Victoria will be regenerate...

registeredInterests.category: 3. Gifts, benefits and hospitality from UK sources

items.text: Name of donor: Randox Laboratories Ltd Address of donor: 55 Diamond Road, Crumlin, Co. Antrim BT29 4QY Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Hospitality for me and a guest at the Grand National, value £938 Date received: 11 April 2026 Date accepted: 11 April 2026 Donor status: company, registration NI015738 (Registered 5 May 2026)

Name of donor: Everton Football Club Limited Address of donor: Hill Dickinson Stadium Bramley Moore Dock Regent Road Liverpool L3 0AP Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Two tickets and hospitality for Everton FC vs Manchester City FC, value £376.30 Date received: 4 May 2026 to 4 May 2026 Date accepted: 4 May 2026 Donor status: company, registration 00036624 (Registered 11 May 2026)

Name of donor: (1) Malaria No More UK (2) Innovative Vector Control Consortium (IVCC) Address of donor: (1) 85 Great Portland Street, First Floor, London W1W 7LT (2) Liverpool School of Tropical Medicine, Pembroke Place, Liverpool, Merseyside L3 5QA. Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): (1) Flights (£2337.62), hotel (£1320.70),internal transport - vehicle hire (£357), internal transport - Fuel & driver (£478.61), food 2 meals per day for 4 days (£200) and visa (£60), value £4,753.93 (2) Meals, 2 per day for 1 day, value £150 Destination of visit: Ghana Dates of visit: 28 July 2025 to 3 August 2025 Purpose of visit: Malaria No More visit to Ghana to see in person some of the lifesaving work and scientific research being carried out by partners who receive funding from the UK. (Registered 9 September 2025)

Voluntary Board Member of SQUASH, Liverpool, a community interest company based in the Liverpool Riverside constituency (a creative food enterprise to empower and enable social change). This is an unpaid role. Date interest ended: 6 November 2025 (Registered 6 January 2020; updated 29 August 2023 and 25 November 2025)

Member of the Liverpool City Region Freeport Board. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 11 April 2022 Date interest ended: 27 September 2024 (Registered 22 April 2022; updated 9 September 2025)
Trustee of the Royal Court Theatre, Liverpool. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 8 April 2022 Date interest ended: 21 February 2025 (Registered 26 April 2022; updated 9 September 2025)
A Member of the British Group Inter-Parliamentary Union Executive Committee. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 30 November 2022 (Registered 20 November 2023)
